name: Potato-Moussaka Casserole
recipeIngredient: 4 medium red skin potatoes (4 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Grease an 8-inch square baking dish or other 2-quart baking dish.
3. Prick the potatoes with a fork. Cook until tender by boiling in a tightly covered saucepan for 10-12 minutes, or in a covered microwave-safe bowl with 2 tablespoons water on high for 5-7 minutes, rearranging potatoes once, until tender. Slice into 1/2-inch thick pieces.
4. Meanwhile, cook the lamb and onion in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat for 5 minutes, stirring to break up meat, until no longer pink. Add 1 cup water, the barley, 1 cup of tomato sauce, oregano, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and cinnamon. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until barley is tender.
5. In a 2-cup glass measure, whisk together milk, eggs, salt, and remaining 1/4 teaspoon pepper until blended.
6. Arrange half the potato slices in a single layer at the bottom of the prepared dish. Spoon the lamb mixture over the potatoes, then top with the remaining potato slices. Pour the milk mixture evenly over the top. Sprinkle with cheese.
7. Bake for 35 minutes or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean and the top is lightly browned. Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
8. Heat remaining tomato sauce in the microwave or a small saucepan. Spoon some on each serving.
Per serving:
483 calories, 24g protein, 49g carbs, 21g fat, 213 cholesterol, 992mg sodium
Exchanges:
2 starch, 3 vegetables, 1.5 medium-fat meat, 2.5 fat.
